question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the amount field in ContractResult to find a record where amount is null versus zero, causing the transaction to be sorted differently in amount-ordered history views and reorganizing the apparent sequence of value-transfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the contractId field in ContractResult to find a record where contractId is zero or a default value, causing the transaction to appear associated with a non-existent contract and reorganizing the per-contract transaction history?
